A2.rule: Used for habits, routines, facts, and general truths
A2.structure: Subject + Base Verb (+ s/es for 3rd person singular)
examples: I drink coffee every morning.
She works at a hospital.
They like pizza.

exercises.question: Complete: He __ to school every day.

A2.rule: Used for actions happening right now or temporary situations
examples: I am studying English.
She is reading a book.
They are playing football.

A2.rule: Used for completed actions in the past
A2.structure: Subject + Past Verb (regular: -ed, irregular: varied forms)
examples: I went to the store yesterday.
She watched a movie last night.
They played tennis yesterday.

exercises.question: Complete: She __ to Paris last summer.

A2.rule: Most nouns add -s or -es to form plural
examples: cat → cats
box → boxes
baby → babies

A2.rule: Used to specify or introduce nouns
examples: I have a dog. (non-specific)
The dog is brown. (specific)
She is an engineer.

B1.rule: Used for actions that started in the past and continue to present, or recent completed actions
examples: I have lived here for 5 years.
She has finished her homework.
They have been to Japan.

exercises.question: Complete: How long __ you __ English?
exercises.explanation: Present perfect shows duration from past to now

B1.rule: Used to express hypothetical situations and their results
examples: If I study hard, I will pass the exam. (First Conditional)
If I studied hard, I would pass the exam. (Second Conditional)
If I had studied hard, I would have passed the exam. (Third Conditional)

exercises.question: Complete: If it rains tomorrow, we __ the picnic.

examples: She is taller than him. (comparative)
She is the tallest in the class. (superlative)
This is more interesting than that. (comparative)

B1.rule: Used for actions that were happening at a specific time in the past
examples: I was reading when she arrived.
They were playing football yesterday.

B1.rule: Used to express plans or predictions
B1.structure: Subject + am/is/are + going to + base verb
examples: I am going to study tomorrow.
She is going to travel next month.

B2.rule: Used for actions that continued over a period before another past action
examples: She had been working for hours when he arrived.
They had been studying all day before the exam.

exercises.question: Complete: By the time the doctor arrived, the patient __ for two hours.
exercises.explanation: Past perfect continuous shows duration before a past event

examples: If I had studied, I would know this. (past condition, present result)
If I were rich, I would have bought a house. (hypothetical past)

B2.rule: Emphasizes the action rather than the actor
B2.structure: Subject + auxiliary verb + past participle (+ by agent)
examples: The book was written by Shakespeare.
The work will be completed tomorrow.
This building was constructed in 1990.

B2.rule: Changes word order for emphasis or formality
examples: Never have I seen such beauty. (instead of I have never seen...)
Only then did I understand. (instead of I understood only then)

C1.topic: Advanced Use of Gerunds and Infinitives
C1.rule: Distinguishes between gerund and infinitive usage
C1.structure: Verb + gerund/infinitive depending on the verb
examples: I prefer reading to watching TV.
He promised to arrive on time.
They insisted on leaving immediately.

C1.rule: Expresses wishes, demands, suggestions, and hypothetical situations
C1.structure: (That) + subject + base verb / were for hypothetical
examples: I suggest that he study harder.
If I were you, I would accept the offer.
